questions.question: Differentiate between a full binary tree and a complete binary tree.
expectedPoints: Full binary tree: every node has 0 or 2 children
Complete binary tree: all levels are completely filled except possibly the last level, which is filled from left to right
All complete binary trees are full, but not all full binary trees are complete

questions.question: Explain the process of deleting a node from a binary search tree that has two children, and discuss why this operation maintains the BST property.
expectedPoints: Identify the node to be deleted
Find the in-order successor (smallest in right subtree) or in-order predecessor (largest in left subtree)
Replace the node's value with the successor's value
Delete the successor node (which has at most one child)
Explain how this maintains the BST property: successor is greater than all left subtree nodes and less than all right subtree nodes
Time complexity: O(h) where h is the height of the tree
